title: flotilla project.yml v1
description: Schema for project.yml v1. See docs/ARCHITECTURE.md §3 for the rationale of each field. This schema is the canonical contract; the Go parser dispatches on the `version` field at the top of the file.

version.description: Schema version. Must be 1 for this schema. The parser uses this to select which validation rules to apply.

name.description: Project identifier. Lowercase letters, digits, underscores, hyphens; must start with a letter. Used in logs, status output, .flotilla/state.json, and as the Traefik router/service base-name when auto-cert is active (underscores are replaced with hyphens in the Traefik name).

domain.description: Primary HTTPS hostname. Lowercase ASCII only. Used for the `Host()` Traefik rule, for ${domain} template expansion in the built-in smoke test, and for matching the project's routers in the Traefik API. IDN domains must be passed in punycode form. Overall length is constrained by minLength/maxLength.

path.description: Absolute path on the VPS where this project lives. flotilla maintains symlinks here for compose.yml and .env.example. Explicit; no `/opt/${name}` default.

description.description: Optional human-readable summary. Surfaces in `flotilla status` listings.

https_entrypoint.description: Optional. When set, flotilla generates the Traefik labels for the named compose service at deploy time. Mutually exclusive with hand-written traefik.* labels on that service (enforced by lint rule L008).

oneOf.description: Shortcut form: a compose service name. The port is inferred from compose; if the service has multiple `expose`/`ports` entries, the deploy fails and the explicit form below is required.
Explicit form. Required when the compose service has multiple expose/ports entries.
